rules.message: A SQL string is built by concatenating an expression into the statement text — if the concatenated part is request-derived, this is SQL injection (the input rewrites the query); even a constant fragment hides that risk from review. Use a parameterized/bound query instead (PreparedStatement `?`, JPA `:named` + setParameter) — never escape values into the string. Suppress a vetted case with `// sql-taint-ok`.

Weak/deprecated cryptography in use — MD5 and SHA-1 have practical collision attacks (forgeable signatures/certificates), DES/RC4 are brute-forceable or biased, and ECB mode leaks plaintext structure across blocks. Use SHA-256 or stronger for hashing and AES-GCM for encryption; for passwords specifically use bcrypt/PBKDF2/Argon2 (a fast hash is the wrong tool even at SHA-256). Suppress a vetted non-security use (e.g. a legacy-interop checksum) with `// weak-crypto-ok`.

Method runs an external process (Runtime.exec / ProcessBuilder) and builds the command by string concatenation — command-injection risk. Pass arguments as an array element per token and validate/allow-list any user-controlled part. Suppress a vetted case with `// cmd-injection-ok`.
